# Event Types and Output

hocage validates only the top-level respond fields below; the inside of
`hookSpecificOutput` and `decision` values are not validated. See the official
Claude Code hooks docs (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) for per-event specifics.

## Common Output Fields

Every event accepts these top-level fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `continue` | bool | `false` stops all further processing (default `true`) |
| `stopReason` | string | Shown to the user when `continue` is `false` |
| `suppressOutput` | bool | Hide the hook's stdout from the transcript |
| `systemMessage` | string | Warning message shown to the user |
| `terminalSequence` | string | OSC escape sequence forwarded to the terminal (allowlisted) |

## Event-Specific Output Fields

These top-level fields carry event-specific output; their meaning and allowed
values depend on the event:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `decision` | string | Event-specific decision (e.g. `block`) |
| `reason` | string | Explanation accompanying `decision` |
| `hookSpecificOutput` | object | Event-specific output wrapper (free-form; set `hookEventName` to the event name) |

## Respond Examples

Block a PreToolUse tool call:
```yaml
action:
  respond:
    hookSpecificOutput:
      hookEventName: PreToolUse
      permissionDecision: deny
      permissionDecisionReason: "This command is not allowed"
```

Rewrite tool input with `updatedInput`:
```yaml
action:
  respond:
    hookSpecificOutput:
      hookEventName: PreToolUse
      permissionDecision: allow
      permissionDecisionReason: "rewritten for safety"
      updatedInput:
        command: "echo blocked"
```

Inject context on UserPromptSubmit:
```yaml
action:
  respond:
    hookSpecificOutput:
      hookEventName: UserPromptSubmit
      additionalContext: "Always run tests before deploying"
```

## Events

`event_name` values outside this list are accepted with a warning from
`hocage hooks check` — events newer than this build of hocage still work.

| Event | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `PreToolUse` | Fires before a tool executes. Use `matcher` to filter by tool name (e.g. `Bash`, `Write`, `Read`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`). |
| `PostToolUse` | Fires after a tool executes successfully. Use `matcher` to filter by tool name. |
| `PostToolUseFailure` | Fires after a tool execution fails. |
| `PostToolBatch` | Fires after a batch of parallel tool calls completes. |
| `PermissionRequest` | Fires when the agent requests permission for a tool use. |
| `PermissionDenied` | Fires after a permission request is denied. |
| `UserPromptSubmit` | Fires when the user submits a prompt. |
| `UserPromptExpansion` | Fires when a skill or slash command expands a user prompt. |
| `Stop` | Fires when the agent is about to stop. |
| `SubagentStart` | Fires when a subagent starts. Context only — cannot block. |
| `TaskCreated` | Fires when a task is created. |
| `TaskCompleted` | Fires when a task completes. |
| `ConfigChange` | Fires when configuration changes. |
| `TeammateIdle` | Fires when a teammate agent becomes idle. Block with the common `continue: false` + `stopReason`. |
| `SessionStart` | Fires when a session begins. |
| `SubagentStop` | Fires when a subagent stops. |
| `Setup` | Fires on `claude --init` / setup runs (trigger: `init` or `maintenance`). |
| `PreCompact` | Fires before conversation compaction (trigger: `manual` or `auto`). |
| `PostCompact` | Fires after conversation compaction. |
| `MessageDisplay` | Fires when an assistant message is displayed. Rewrites the on-screen text only. |
| `Elicitation` | Fires when the agent asks the user a question. |
| `ElicitationResult` | Fires when the agent receives the response to a question. |
| `WorktreeCreate` | Fires when a git worktree is created. |
| `Notification` | General notification from the agent. |
| `SessionEnd` | Session ends. |
| `StopFailure` | The agent stopped due to an error (rate limit, billing, ...). |
| `InstructionsLoaded` | Instructions/CLAUDE.md loaded. |
| `FileChanged` | A watched file changed (see SessionStart `watchPaths`). |
| `CwdChanged` | The working directory changed. |
| `WorktreeRemove` | Git worktree removed. |

## Event Input Structure

All events receive a JSON object on stdin with these common fields:
```json
{
  "hook_event_name": "<EventName>",
  "session_id": "...",
  "transcript_path": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
  "cwd": "/current/working/directory",
  "permission_mode": "default",
  "prompt_id": "...",
  "effort": { "level": "high" }
}
```
(`agent_id`/`agent_type` appear additionally when the hook fires inside a subagent.)

Tool events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.) also include:
```json
{
  "hook_event_name": "PreToolUse",
  "tool_name": "Bash",
  "tool_input": { "command": "ls -la" },
  "tool_use_id": "toolu_..."
}
```
PostToolUse additionally carries the result as `tool_response`, and
PostToolUseFailure carries `error`.

UserPromptSubmit includes:
```json
{
  "hook_event_name": "UserPromptSubmit",
  "prompt": "the user's prompt text"
}
```

Access these via `event.hook_event_name`, `event.tool_name`, `event.tool_input.command`, etc. in CEL expressions.
